citation_insertions.allowed_claim: VF-H2 uses graph-like discrete state-update language.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: VF-H2 is validated or formally equivalent to parallel dynamical systems.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Graph Dynamical Systems and Discrete Network Dynamics

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Finite dynamical systems are relevant background for discrete simulation frameworks.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: The source validates VF-H2 or the Viruse Fabric theory.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Graph Dynamical Systems and Discrete Network Dynamics

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Temporal-network literature provides conceptual background for time-indexed network processes.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: The source validates the VF-H2 three-time coordinate.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Temporal Networks and Multi-Time Indexing

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Network memory can be structured, motivating careful treatment of memory mechanisms.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: The source validates persistent memory ledger or ledger_effect_size.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Memory and Non-Markovian Processes in Networks

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Higher-order network models are relevant conceptual background for history-sensitive network processes.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: VF-H2 is a higher-order network model or is validated by this source.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Memory and Non-Markovian Processes in Networks

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Memory can alter dynamics in cellular automata, useful as toy/rule-system context.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: VF-H2 is a cellular automaton or is formally equivalent to one.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Memory-Augmented Automata and Rule-Based Toy Systems

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Reservoir-style systems offer memory-bearing recurrent-state vocabulary.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: VF-H2 is an echo state network or reservoir computing model.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Reservoir-Style Memory and Recurrent State Analogies

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Memory capacity is a term used in echo-state/reservoir contexts.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Reservoir-Style Memory and Recurrent State Analogies

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Null models can be used as methodological comparison tools.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: Null models prove VF-H2 or validate the no-persistent-memory null control.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Null Models, Ablation, Robustness, and Artifact Checks

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Reproducibility is a recognized concern in computational research.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: VF-H2 has external replication or empirical validation.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Null Models, Ablation, Robustness, and Artifact Checks

citation_insertions.allowed_claim: Structured robustness assessment is a relevant methodological concern.
citation_insertions.forbidden_claim: VF-H2 has independent empirical validation or field-level replication.
citation_insertions.target_section: Related Work / Null Models, Ablation, Robustness, and Artifact Checks

references_text:
# Verified Related-Work References v1

## Status Boundary

This reference list belongs only to the citation-inserted related-work draft. It does not create manuscript readiness, does not claim literature validation, and does not validate VF-H2.

## References

Aledo, J. A., Martinez, S., & Valverde, J. C. (2015). Parallel dynamical systems over graphs and related topics: A survey. *Journal of Applied Mathematics*, 2015, Article ID 594294, 1-14. DOI: 10.1155/2015/594294.

Jarrah, A. S., & Laubenbacher, R. (2008). Finite dynamical systems: A mathematical framework for computer simulation. In *Mathematical Modeling, Simulation, Visualization and e-Learning* (pp. 343-358). DOI: 10.1007/978-3-540-74339-2_21.

Holme, P., & Saramäki, J. (2012). Temporal networks. *Physics Reports*, 519(3), 97-125. DOI: 10.1016/j.physrep.2012.03.001.

Williams, O. E., Lacasa, L., Millán, A. P., Latora, V., et al. (2022). The shape of memory in temporal networks. *Nature Communications*, 13, Article 499. DOI: 10.1038/s41467-022-28123-z.

Lambiotte, R., Rosvall, M., & Scholtes, I. (2019). From networks to optimal higher-order models of complex systems. *Nature Physics*, 15(4), 313-320. DOI: 10.1038/s41567-019-0459-y.

Martínez, G. J., Adamatzky, A., & Alonso-Sanz, R. (2013). Designing complex dynamics in cellular automata with memory. *International Journal of Bifurcation and Chaos*, 23(10), 1330035. DOI: 10.1142/S0218127413300358.

Jaeger, H. (2007). Echo state network. *Scholarpedia*, 2(9), 2330. DOI: 10.4249/scholarpedia.2330.

Barančok, P., & Farkaš, I. (2014). Memory capacity of input-driven echo state networks at the edge of chaos. In *Artificial Neural Networks and Machine Learning - ICANN 2014*, LNCS 8681, 41-48. DOI: 10.1007/978-3-319-11179-7_6.

Váša, F., & Mišić, B. (2022). Null models in network neuroscience. *Nature Reviews Neuroscience*, 23(8), 493-504. DOI: 10.1038/s41583-022-00601-9.

Nature Computational Science Editorial Board. (2021). Moving towards reproducible machine learning. *Nature Computational Science*, 1, 629-630. DOI: 10.1038/s43588-021-00152-6.

Ankel-Peters, J., et al. (2025). A protocol for structured robustness reproductions and replicability assessments. *Q Open*. DOI: 10.1093/qopen/qoaf004.

## Explicit Non-Claims

This reference list does not claim:

- literature validation,
- theory validation,
- formal proof,
- external validation,
- independent empirical validation,
- biological relevance,
- real-world relevance,
- manuscript readiness,
- submission readiness.

## Safety Boundary

Safe abstract toy model only. No real biological datasets, no real pathogen models, no receptor parameters, no operational targeting, no wet-lab protocol, no infectivity optimization, no immune evasion optimization, and no host range prediction are introduced.
related_work_draft_text:
# Related Work Draft with Verified Citation Keys v1

## Status Boundary

This is a citation-inserted related-work draft only. It is not a complete manuscript, not a submission-ready paper, not a formal proof, not theory validation, not external validation, not independent empirical validation, and not biological validation.

The inserted citation keys are used only for bounded related-work roles: background, terminology support, conceptual positioning, and methodological framing. They do not validate VF-H2, do not validate the broader Viruse Fabric theory, and do not establish biological, real-world, operational, or empirical relevance.

## 1. Graph Dynamical Systems and Discrete Network Dynamics

VF-H2 is framed as a safe abstract toy model over finite graph-like state spaces. The graph dynamical systems literature provides a background vocabulary for discussing graph-based state updates and discrete networked dynamics [@AledoMartinezValverde2015]. Finite dynamical systems also provide general mathematical background for simulation-oriented discrete systems [@JarrahLaubenbacher2008].

This use is conceptual only. These sources do not prove VF-H2, do not validate `ledger_effect_size`, and do not establish formal equivalence between VF-H2 and any existing graph dynamical-system class.

## 2. Memory and Non-Markovian Processes in Networks

VF-H2 uses a persistent memory ledger to distinguish memory-bearing dynamics from no-persistent-memory null controls. Temporal-network and higher-order network literatures provide conceptual background for memory, path dependence, and history-sensitive network representations [@WilliamsLacasaMillanLatora2022; @LambiotteRosvallScholtes2019].

This use is limited to conceptual positioning. These sources do not validate the persistent memory ledger, do not validate the positive toy signal, and do not establish external or empirical support for VF-H2.

## 3. Temporal Networks and Multi-Time Indexing

The three-time coordinate in VF-H2 separates local update time, ledger accumulation time, and projection or observation time. Temporal-network literature provides a cautious background for thinking about time-indexed network processes and dynamic graph structures [@HolmeSaramaki2012].

This use does not validate the VF-H2 three-time coordinate. It only helps position the notation near broader time-indexed network concepts.

## 4. Memory-Augmented Automata and Rule-Based Toy Systems

VF-H2 is a rule-based abstract toy system in which persistent memory can affect update outcomes. Work on cellular automata with memory provides a related conceptual background for how memory can alter the dynamics of discrete rule-based systems [@MartinezAdamatzkyAlonsoSanz2013].

This does not imply that VF-H2 is a cellular automaton, nor does it establish formal equivalence to cellular automata with memory.

## 5. Reservoir-Style Memory and Recurrent State Analogies

Reservoir-style systems and echo state networks provide terminology-level background for memory-bearing recurrent state and memory capacity [@Jaeger2007; @BarancokFarkas2014]. VF-H2 is not presented as a reservoir computing model, and the analogy is intentionally limited.

These citations are used only for terminology support and analogy-level positioning. They do not imply that VF-H2 has reservoir-computing memory capacity.

## 6. Null Models, Ablation, Robustness, and Artifact Checks

VF-H2 uses no-persistent-memory null controls, ablation checks, artifact-resistance checks, and independent implementation-level replication to reduce internal toy-artifact risk. Methodological literature on null models and reproducibility can frame these choices as cautious computational practice [@VasaMisic2022; @NatureComputationalScienceEditorial2021; @AnkelPetersEtAl2025].

This methodological framing does not prove VF-H2. It does not create external validation, independent empirical validation, manuscript readiness, or submission readiness.

## 7. Excluded or Held Sources

The candidate source on non-Markovian epidemic spreading is excluded from the current related-work draft because epidemic-spreading language creates unnecessary biological and real-world boundary risk.

The book candidate on cellular automata with memory and the artificial-life overview candidate remain held for additional bibliographic or scope review. They are not inserted here.

## 8. Related-Work Boundary

The related-work draft supports only bounded positioning. No citation in this draft may be interpreted as:

- VF-H2 validation,
- Viruse Fabric theory validation,
- formal proof,
- empirical validation,
- biological validation,
- external validation,
- manuscript readiness,
- submission readiness,
- real-world applicability,
- operational usefulness.

## 9. Safety Boundary

Safe abstract toy model only. No real biological datasets, no real pathogen models, no receptor parameters, no operational targeting, no wet-lab protocol, no infectivity optimization, no immune evasion optimization, and no host range prediction are introduced.
